2605.18635.title: Data Presentation Over Architecture: Resampling Strategies for Credit Risk Prediction with Tabular Foundation Models
2605.18635.paper_summary: Credit default prediction is a tabular learning problem with severe class imbalance, heterogeneous features, and tight latency budgets. Tabular Foundation Models (TFMs) approach this problem through in-context learning, which makes their predictions sensitive to how the context window is built. We benchmark four classical models and five TFMs on the Home Credit and Lending Club datasets, varying the context-construction strategy (seven options) and the context size (1K to 50K). On both datasets, the choice of context strategy explains more variance in AUC-ROC than the choice of TFM family: balanced and hybrid sampling add 3 to 4 AUC points over uniform sampling, and the gap exceeds the spread between TFMs. With a balanced context of 5K to 10K examples, the strongest TFMs reach the AUC of classical baselines trained on the full data, while also recovering meaningful default-class recall that default-threshold GBDTs do not. We frame this as evidence that context construction, rather than architecture choice, is the primary deployment lever for TFMs in imbalanced credit-risk settings.
2605.18635.paper_summary_zh: 信用違約預測是一個具有嚴重類別不平衡、異質特徵和緊湊延遲預算的表格學習問題。<lb/>表格基礎模型（TFMs）通過上下文學習來解決這個問題，使得它們的預測對上下文窗口的構建方式非常敏感。<lb/>我們在 Home Credit 和 Lending Club 數據集上對四個經典模型和五個 TFMs 進行基準測試，變化上下文構建策略（七個選項）和上下文大小（1K 到 50K）。<lb/>在這兩個數據集中，上下文策略的選擇在 AUC-ROC 中解釋的變異性超過了 TFM 家族的選擇：平衡和混合抽樣比均勻抽樣增加了 3 到 4 個 AUC 點，這個差距超過了 TFMs 之間的差異。<lb/>在 5K 到 10K 範例的平衡上下文下，最強的 TFMs 達到了在完整數據上訓練的經典基準的 AUC，同時也恢復了有意義的違約類別召回率，而違約閾值 GBDTs 則沒有。<lb/>我們將此視為證據，表明上下文構建，而非架構選擇，是 TFMs 在不平衡信用風險環境中的主要部署杠杆。
2605.18635.author: Aditya Tanna et.al.
2605.18635.authors: Aditya Tanna, Mitul Solanki, Mohamed Bouadi, Nassim Bouarour, Pratinav Seth, Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
